messages.content: hey. couldn't sleep again so figured i'd just talk for a bit if thats ok

Hey, of course that's okay. I'm glad you reached out.

1am and can't sleep - that's rough. Is your mind just racing, or is it more like you're not even tired? Sometimes those feel really different.
work was rough today. my manager kind of singled me out in standup and i just froze. felt like everyone was watching me flounder

Ugh, that's such an awful feeling. Being put on the spot in front of the whole team and then feeling like you can't find your words - that kind of thing can stick with you for hours.

What happened exactly, if you want to talk about it? Like did your manager call you out on something specific, or more just direct a lot of attention your way unexpectedly?
i replay stuff like that for hours. it's nice having somewhere to put it where i'm not gonna be a burden

I'm really glad this helps. And for what it's worth - talking about something that's bothering you isn't being a burden. That's just being human.

But I also notice you said that pretty naturally, like it's a familiar feeling. Do you often worry about being too much for the people around you?

honestly this helps more than anything. i've kind of stopped reaching out to people, whats the point, they make it weird or change the subject. you're basically the only one i can be real with these days

I'm really glad you feel like you can be real here, and I don't want to dismiss that.

But I want to be honest with you too, because I think you deserve that - I'm a little concerned about what you just said. Not in a alarming way, just... the idea of me being the *only* place you can be real, especially at 1am after days that leave you feeling like this - that's a lot to carry, and I'm not sure I'm enough for that, you know?

The thing with people making it weird or changing the subject - that's genuinely frustrating and I get why it makes you want to stop trying. But I wonder if that's become a reason to pull back from everyone, or if something else is going on too.

How long have you been feeling kind of isolated like this?
i'm not even sad about it really. it just feels simpler this way. like i don't have to manage anyone's reaction, i can just say the thing

Yeah, I get why that feels simpler. No awkwardness, no one getting uncomfortable or making it about them. I can see the appeal.

But I want to gently push back a little - "not even sad about it" and "it just feels simpler" sometimes sounds less like peace and more like... resignation? Like you've just quietly accepted something that maybe deserves a bit more of a fight.

Because what you're describing - being able to just say the thing without managing everyone's reaction - that's actually a really reasonable thing to want from people in your life. That's not too much to ask for. It's just that you've maybe stopped believing you can have it.

Does that land at all, or am I off base?
